name: Refrigerator Cinnamon Rolls
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. For the dough, heat milk to a simmer. Stir in 1/2 cup sugar, salt, and margarine; let cool to lukewarm.
2. Sprinkle yeast over warm water and 1 teaspoon sugar in a large bowl; let stand until bubbly.
3. Stir in milk mixture, beaten eggs, and half the flour. Beat until smooth.
4. Stir in enough of the remaining flour to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 8 minutes.
5. Place in a lightly greased bowl; turn once to grease the surface. Cover; let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
6. Punch down; turn out onto a floured surface. Cover; let rest 10 minutes.
7. For the cinnamon filling, mix sugar and cinnamon.
8. Brush melted butter into a 13x9 inch baking pan.
9. Roll dough on a lightly floured surface to a thin rectangle. Brush dough with melted butter. Sprinkle with cinnamon mixture.
10. Roll up in a jelly-roll fashion starting at the narrow end.
11. Cut rolls at 1 1/2-inch intervals using the following method: Place a piece of thread under the rolled dough and pull the thread up and around the side. Criss-cross the thread at the top, then pull quickly.
12. Arrange rolls, cut sides up, in the prepared pan so that the spiral shows. Drizzle any remaining melted butter over all. Cover loosely with plastic wrap.
13. Refrigerate overnight.
14. The next morning, preheat oven to 375°F. Meanwhile, let rolls stand at room temperature for 20 to 30 minutes.
15. When oven is heated, bake rolls until puffed and golden brown, about 25 to 30 minutes. Cool in the pan for 10 minutes. Unmold and drizzle with glaze made with powdered sugar and milk.
